name: Unguarded Non-Null Assertion
description: TypeScript non-null assertion operator (!) used without a preceding null/undefined check in the path conditions.

teachingExample.explanation: regex match result indexed with ! but match can return null
teachingExample.smt2:
(declare-const match_is_null Int)
(declare-const checked Int)
(assert (= checked 0))
(assert (= match_is_null 1))
(check-sat)

smt2ProofTemplate:
(declare-const {{value}}_is_null Int)
; Guard: path condition checks for null
(assert (= {{value}}_is_null 0))
; Can value still be null?
(assert (= {{value}}_is_null 1))
(check-sat)
; unsat → proven: null check guards the assertion
